The Scnn1a gene encodes the alpha subunit of the epithelial sodium channel (ENaC). Homozygous knockout of Scnn1a (tm1Rss) in mice results in neonatal lethality. These mice fail to clear lung fluid after birth, leading to respiratory distress, cyanosis, and death within the first few days of life. This is a well-documented finding in the literature (Hummler et al., 1996).

This is a form of premature death (embryonic lethality), which clearly impairs survival.
